A cultural center of this copper-working people is found at Kay Llnagay, near their main town of Skidegate. A northern population of these people known as the Kaigani are traditional adversaries of the Tongass, who refer to them as the “far out to sea people.” For 10 points, name this Athabascan people of southern Prince of Wales Island as well as a namesake island formerly known as the Queen Charlotte Archipelago, known for their tall totems.

Lafcadio Hearn's Kwaidan includes the first description of a Japanese woman made of this substance. This substance also titles a Yukio Mishima novel in which Kiyoaki has a forbidden love for Satoko.
Hearn was born in this country. Other writers from here include a man who fictionalized the dying moments of Jesus Christ on the cross in The Last Temptation of Christ, Nikos Kazantzakis.

bonuses.leadin: One of the better-known kings of this city, Eshmunazar II, was the son of king Tabnit and lends his name to a sarcophagus now housed at the Louvre. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this ancient port city whose name roughly translates as “fishery.”

Sidon was a city within this ancient Mediterranean civilization, which also had centers at Byblos and Tyre. In Greek, this region’s name means “land of purple,” in reference to its famed purple dye of the murex snail.

The conquering and destruction of Phoenicia is described by this Greek historian in his Bibliotheca Historica written around 50 BC. He also wrote about the legend of the Assyrian queen Semiramis and, as his surname indicates, he hailed from Sicily.
answers_sanitized: Sidon [or Saida or Sayda]
Phoenicians
Diodorus Siculus [Diodorus of Sicily]

bonuses.leadin: “It ain’t over ‘till the fat lady sings.” – For 10 points each, three bonuses on opera.
parts: At the end of which grand opera does the heroine, a captive Ethiopian princess, choose to die with her lover, entombed beneath a pyramid ?

La Wally
